title: Forest Fire Inspectors and Prevention Specialists
description: Enforce fire regulations, inspect forest for fire hazards, and recommend forest fire prevention or control measures. May report forest fires and weather conditions.

tasks.text: Relay messages about emergencies, accidents, locations of crew and personnel, and fire hazard conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Estimate sizes and characteristics of fires, and report findings to base camps by radio or telephone.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct crews working on firelines during forest fires.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Locate forest fires on area maps, using azimuth sighters and known landmarks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Extinguish smaller fires with portable extinguishers, shovels, and axes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Patrol assigned areas, looking for forest fires, hazardous conditions, and weather phenomena.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compile and report meteorological data, such as temperature, relative humidity, wind direction and velocity, and types of cloud formations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine and inventory firefighting equipment, such as axes, fire hoses, shovels, pumps, buckets, and fire extinguishers, to determine amount and condition.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Educate the public about fire safety and prevention.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct maintenance and repair of firefighting equipment, or requisition new equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Administer regulations regarding sanitation, fire prevention, violation corrections, and related forest regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Restrict public access and recreational use of forest lands during critical fire seasons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect camp sites to ensure that campers are in compliance with forest use regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Inspect forest tracts and logging areas for fire hazards such as accumulated wastes or mishandling of combustibles, and recommend appropriate fire prevention measures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
